attacking_style: Long-range solo attacks from 1km-40km out, primarily on descents or late in climbs. Shows willingness to bridge large gaps mid-race (51 seconds) and sustain efforts over extended distances rather than explosive final-kilometer moves.
team_tactics: Tactical role frequently unclear or conflicting—sacrifices for leaders (Almeida, Valverde) but also attacks independently causing team friction. UAE allows stage-hunting freedom even when holding GC positions. Minimal visible domestique support when defending leader jerseys.
terrain_preference: Technical descents where he launches decisive attacks (Alto San Miguel descent win). Sustained climbs over 40km suit better than explosive steep gradients. Home roads in Catalunya/Spain show marked performance boost. Struggles on moderate gradients and flat echelon stages.
vulnerability: Extreme form volatility—swings from missing Tour time limits by 30 minutes to winning Vuelta stages weeks later. Dropped early on moderate climbs (10km+ out) when form is off. Technical descent handling inconsistent despite descent attack success. Echelon/crosswind splits expose positioning weaknesses.
key_patterns: Long-range breakaway specialist: multiple wins from 1km-40km solo efforts, bridging mid-race gaps
Form volatility extreme: Tour DNF to Vuelta winner in weeks, complete GC collapses without incidents
Descent attacks signature move: won from 1km descent attack, home roads descending advantage noted
Team role confusion chronic: attacked away from leaders 4+ times, unclear hierarchy with teammates
Stage hunter over GC: third-place Vuelta representation confirms UAE prioritizes stages over sustained GC focus
Recovery issues targeting multiple stages: stage 5 winner couldn't sustain later mountain efforts same race
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 showed both Tour stage win and sudden Vuelta GC collapse from second place without incident, continuing extreme volatility pattern rather than clear upward or downward trend
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 17
